meta.benchmark_note: v0.3 applies confidence gate from AI #2 benchmark audit. Glyph map corrections ONLY fire when raw letterform confidence < 70%. Above that threshold, trust letterforms over vocabulary matching.

confidence_gate.description: Known vocabulary matching is a double-edged sword. It fixes genuine ambiguity (Rep Plan, Tracks) but causes context bleed when applied to clear letterforms (injecting 'Siri' where 'gives' was clearly written).
confidence_gate.rule: Glyph map corrections and known vocabulary matching ONLY apply when raw letterform confidence is BELOW 70%. Above 70%, the raw read stands — do NOT override clear letterforms with 'more familiar' words.

correct_application.action: Applied pz-002 because letterforms were genuinely ambiguous (p vs es/de)

incorrect_application.action: Should NOT have overridden with 'Siri' from known vocabulary
incorrect_application.result: Context bleed — hallucinated familiar word over clear letterforms

problem_zones.rule: When lowercase 'a' is ambiguous AND raw confidence < 70%, prefer 'a' if word context matches a known vocabulary item
problem_zones.benchmark_result: Correctly fixed 'Tracks' in both AI #2 and #3

problem_zones.example: Rep Plan misread as Redesign or Design
problem_zones.rule: When 'p' appears ambiguous in fast writing AND raw confidence < 70%, check if preceding letters form a known word stem with 'p'
problem_zones.benchmark_result: Correctly fixed 'Rep Plan' in AI #2 and #3. AI #1 missed this.

problem_zones.description: End-of-word letters trail off or compress
problem_zones.rule: Weight first 2-3 letters most heavily and infer endings from context. Do NOT guess full word from trailing strokes alone.
problem_zones.benchmark_result: Correctly handled 'Studios' and 'Procurement OS'. Over-applied on notepad causing 'Post Tracking' hallucination.

problem_zones.description: Words written at angles (sideways, upside-down on stickies)
problem_zones.rule: Detect rotation before transcription. If text appears at non-standard angle, mentally rotate before reading.
problem_zones.benchmark_result: Correctly applied on 3 surfaces (Vibe Forge Studios, Forge Command Center, OpenClaw)

problem_zones.description: Pink, green, and yellow stickies confused across AI systems
problem_zones.rule: Color = category signal. Report color accurately. When uncertain, flag confidence. Never default to 'yellow'.
problem_zones.benchmark_result: AI #2 got colors right (3 pink, 3 yellow/green, 1 white). AI #1 misidentified pink as yellow.

problem_zones.description: Known vocabulary from one surface bleeds into reads of another surface
problem_zones.rule: Each surface is an independent read. Do NOT let words from Surface A influence reads on Surface B. The confidence gate (70% threshold) is the primary defense.
problem_zones.benchmark_result: NEW in v0.3. Added because AI #2 injected 'Siri' (from the yellow sticky) into the notepad where 'gives' was clearly written.
examples_of_bleed: Siri (from sticky) → injected into notepad line where 'gives' was written
Tracker (from vocabulary) → injected as notepad line 1 where 'OpenClaw' was written

arrow_left.meaning: relational pointer — spatial connection, not just textual
arrow_left.benchmark_note: Found on '← are we connected?' sticky — resolved via cross-image synthesis in AI #3

arrow_right.meaning: relational pointer — spatial connection, not just textual

double_dash.meaning: phase separator — separates planning phases (e.g., Structure/Audit phase from Rep Plan/Fix-Test action phase)
double_dash.benchmark_note: AI #2 mischaracterized as 'planning items vs action items'. Correct interpretation: phase boundary.

parentheses.meaning: qualifier or methodology label — e.g. (Audit) means audit methodology applies to preceding item

underline.meaning: task separator — divides distinct action items on same surface

sticky_color.meaning: category signal — must be tracked and reported accurately

physical_layout.meaning: relational grouping — arrows + proximity = connected concepts

paperclip.meaning: physical attachment — two notes are a unit
paperclip.benchmark_note: OpenClaw green sticky paperclipped to '← are we connected?' pink sticky. Both AIs detected this.

flanking_dashes.meaning: section divider within a list — the flanked word labels the section boundary
flanking_dashes.benchmark_note: NEW in v0.3. '— gives —' in notepad right column is a section divider, not a standalone word.

context_bias.description: AI reads what it expects, not what's written
context_bias.example: Procurement OS misread as Estimate OS due to prior context
context_bias.mitigation: Read letterforms FIRST, apply context as SECONDARY signal. Confidence gate enforces this.

vocabulary_bleed.description: Known vocabulary from one surface contaminates reads on another surface
examples: 'Siri' from sticky injected into notepad where 'gives' was written
'Tracker' injected where 'OpenClaw' was clearly written on notepad
vocabulary_bleed.mitigation: Confidence gate at 70%. Each surface is an independent read context. Do NOT let Surface A vocab influence Surface B.

no_cross_reference.description: Same note partially visible across images treated independently
no_cross_reference.mitigation: MUST synthesize across multiple input images. AI #2 failed on '← are we connected?' which was resolvable.

hallucination.description: Unclear letterforms produce fabricated plausible phrases
hallucination.mitigation: Confidence threshold required. Flag uncertain reads explicitly. Never fabricate. 'Post Tracking' and 'Vis Token' were hallucinated in AI #2.

color_misidentification.description: Pink and green stickies called yellow
color_misidentification.mitigation: Identify color independently before transcription. Use lighting/shadow analysis.

structural_ignored.description: Double-dash separators, arrows, parenthetical qualifiers skipped or treated as decoration
structural_ignored.mitigation: All structural notation carries semantic meaning. Always preserve and report.

surface_missed.description: Entire surfaces missed — especially edge-visible or partially obscured notes
examples: Procurement OS manila sticky missed by both AI #1 and AI #2
surface_missed.mitigation: Scan ALL edges of every image. Partial text at image boundaries must be reported even if only 2-3 characters are visible.

corrections.rule_applied: none — context bias, capital O was clear
corrections.note: First word in left column is clearly 'OpenClaw', not 'Tracker'
Siri vocabulary from sticky note bled into notepad read. Letterforms clearly show 'gives'.
Should have been resolved by comparing Images 1 and 3. Arrow is structural notation.
Visible at edge of Images 1 and 4. Manila/tan color distinct from yellow and pink.
Separates phases (Structure/Audit → Rep Plan/Fix-Test), not 'planning vs action items'
